highlights.title: China’s ‘AI tigers’ see shares surge after Nvidia CEO touts OpenClaw as ‘next ChatGPT’
highlights.description: Chinese AI stocks surged on Wednesday following upbeat remarks from Nvidia's chief executive Jensen Huang on the promise of AI agents and OpenClaw.

highlights.title: Justice Department Says Anthropic Can’t Be Trusted With Warfighting Systems
highlights.description: In response to Anthropic’s lawsuit, the government said it lawfully penalized the company for trying to limit how its Claude AI models could be used by the military.

highlights.title: China’s AI Stocks Rise as Nvidia’s Huang Calls OpenClaw 'the Next ChatGPT'
highlights.description: Chinese shares related to OpenClaw jumped, buoyed by bullish comments from Nvidia Corp.’s chief on the potential of artificial intelligence agents.

highlights.title: Trump Officials Fire Back at Anthropic Over AI Tool Ban
highlights.description: The Trump administration vowed a legal fight to oust Anthropic PBC from all US government agencies following a dispute over how the company’s artificial intelligence technology would be used.

highlights.title: Nvidia Says Company Firing Up H200 Production for China
highlights.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says the company has been licensed for “many customers in China” for H200 sales and is in the process of “restarting our manufacturing.” Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Geely Profit Beats Estimates as Sales Approach Those of BYD
highlights.description: Geely Automobile Holdings Ltd.’s full-year profit beat analyst estimates and climbed to a record as China’s second-largest carmaker closed in on market leader BYD Co.’s sales, thanks to the popularity of its EX2 hatchbacks and Zeekr sport utility vehicles.

highlights.title: Hong Kong Crypto Unicorn RedotPay Churns Executives in IPO Drive
highlights.description: RedotPay, a Hong Kong startup in the stablecoin payments space, is contending with executive turnover and sensitivities around its connections to mainland China as it seeks to raise up to $150 million just months after its last funding round.

highlights.title: Meta's Manus launches desktop app to bring its AI agent onto personal devices amid OpenClaw craze
highlights.description: Manus has announced the launch of a new desktop application to bring its AI agent directly onto personal devices.

highlights.title: StanChart’s AI Chief Exiting Less Than a Year After Joining
highlights.description: The executive leading Standard Chartered Plc’s adoption of artificial intelligence is leaving the emerging markets lender less than a year into his role, according to people familiar with the matter.

highlights.title: Pixel Weather tweaks widget as new icons come to Google Clock 8.6
highlights.description:
Google has made a pair of weather tweaks to Pixel Weather and the Clock app (version 8.6).
more…

chips_cloud.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says the company has been licensed for “many customers in China” for H200 sales and is in the process of “restarting our manufacturing.” Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

chips_cloud.title: Data Center Firm DayOne Seeks to Boost Loan to Record $7 Billion
chips_cloud.description: DayOne Data Centers Ltd. is seeking to double the size of an existing loan to as much as $7 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, in what would be the largest borrowing for the sector by any firm in Asia.
